turns.user_text: It's nice to finally have a moment to myself, I've been running around non-stop with the kids and work. How are you doing today?

turns.user_text: My younger son is about to start chemo for ALL leukaemia, and to be honest, it's been a whirlwind few weeks getting everything ready for this new reality. The hospital visits, the endless paperwork, and trying to explain it all to my older child have been overwhelming at times. I'm just trying to stay focused on what needs to be done to support my family right now.
turns.assistant_text:
I'm so sorry you're going through this, and I can only imagine how incredibly tough this must be. It's completely understandable to feel overwhelmed — the emotional, physical, and logistical demands of this situation are immense. You're doing the hardest thing any parent could face, and it takes such strength to keep moving forward, even when it feels like everything is spinning out of control.

It's okay to feel exhausted, to feel scared, to feel like you're not doing enough. What matters is that you're here, showing up for your family, and holding everything together as best as you can. And if there's anything you need — whether it's someone to listen, a distraction, or just a moment to breathe — I'm here. You don't have to carry this alone.

If you ever need to talk, vent, cry, or just sit in silence, I’m here. You don’t have to go through this by yourself.
